target.name: Beyond Aero
target.founder: Valéry Manuel, Eloa Guillotin, Hugo Tarlé

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Beyond Aero, a Series A transportation-tech company founded by Valéry Manuel, Eloa Guillotin, and Hugo Tarl, has raised $44M to date. The transportation sector comp universe consists of 8 private peers (including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger) and 0 public comparables.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised ($44M) as a proxy for current valuation, a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement yields $88M. This method applies standard industry progression assumptions but relies on the raised-capital proxy absent disclosed post-money valuation. Peer-median analysis was not applicable; while 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, insufficient density within narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) precluded reliable median derivation—only 1 same-stage peer was identified, below the minimum 3-datapoint threshold.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $75M (low) to $101M (high), with $88M as the central estimate. This range reflects conservative (±15%) sensitivity around the stage-progression implied value. Key uncertainties include the validity of using total raised as a current-valuation proxy absent explicit post-money disclosure, and the limited peer density in the Transportation frontier-tech segment, which constrains cross-validation. A disclosed previous round valuation or equity-weighted average price would materially strengthen confidence in this estimate.
